title: Dual-Mode Earth-Ionosphere Excitation: Reconciling Tesla's Colorado Springs Observations with Modern Electromagnetic Theory
description: We present 20 computational experiments investigating Tesla's electromagnetic claims through modern physics. Digital reconstruction of Tesla's Colorado Springs magnifying transmitter reveals simultaneous excitation of TM₀ surface wave modes and TE Schumann cavity resonances — a dual-mode coupling mechanism not previously described in the literature. Key findings include: (1) Marconi's 1901 transatlantic signal likely propagated via ground wave, not skywave; (2) Tesla's 'longitudinal waves' correspond to real TM₀ guided mode components; (3) Wardenclyffe Tower would have functioned as a global communication system; (4) the core system is replicable with modern components for ~$2,000.
